# Argument Map

## Claim 1
Digital markets allocate value through proxy signals.

## Claim 2
Proxy signals can be strategically distorted, amplified, or concealed.

## Claim 3
This distortion occurs across many domains, not only social media.

## Claim 4
The common mechanism is signal corruption: outsiders infer genuine demand from engineered evidence.

## Claim 5
The harm is systemic because corrupted signals influence recommendations, rankings, purchases, ad budgets, royalties, hiring, investment, and cultural legitimacy.

## Claim 6
The right ethical boundary is whether a growth tactic improves truthful discoverability or falsifies evidence.

## Claim 7
Regulators and platforms already recognize parts of the boundary; the unresolved problem is detection, attribution, and enforcement across domains.
